executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised in the last 48 hours, including high-profile victims Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 19 victims, followed by Play (11), Clop (13), and SafePay (11). Additionally, CCCS issued Alert AL26-008 for a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940) affecting cPanel and WebHost Manager that requires immediate patching. This represents a significant escalation in targeting across all sectors including public sector, healthcare, education, and financial services.

items.description: Vulnerability affecting cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM)
items.cia_impact: Not specified in advisory - requires further analysis

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces an extreme ransomware surge with over 100 confirmed victims across all sectors in 48 hours, representing one of the largest coordinated campaigns against Canadian infrastructure on record. CCCS issued Alert AL26-008 regarding CVE-2026-41940 affecting cPanel/WHM, requiring immediate attention from hosting providers. Critical infrastructure impacts include public health (Peterborough), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart Institute), municipalities (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network).
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major consumer brand), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail), and Kids & Company (childcare services chain) compromised
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware, Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi, Trucash targeted by Lynx ransomware. Multiple accounting firms also affected including Pewarchuk CPA and MG Chartered Professional Accountant.

report_2w.executive_summary: Canada is experiencing a severe ransomware wave: 13 Canadian organizations were hit in the past two weeks, including major brands Canada Goose (ShinyHunters), law firms Thompson Dorfman Sweatman and McCague Borlack LLP, Collège Supérieur De Montréal, and technology provider GuestTek. Additionally, CCCS issued Alert AL25-012 warning of three critical vulnerabilities in Cisco ASA and FTD devices (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) that require immediate patching. Globally, ShinyHunters claimed a massive breach of Udemy exposing 1.4 million accounts and is also tied to breaches of ADT and Carnival Cruise Lines. The npm ecosystem is under active assault by a new supply-chain worm targeting developer environments.

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
items.cia_impact: Unknown - CCCS advisory indicates urgent patching required

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faced a ransomware crisis with 13 organizations compromised across technology, legal, education, energy, and transportation sectors. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 on critical Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerabilities requiring immediate action. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (fashion retailer), Collège Supérieur De Montréal, law firms Thompson Dorfman Sweatman and McCague Borlack LLP, and technology providers Salesfloor, GuestTek, and SpeedLine Solutions.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose - compromised by ShinyHunters ransomware group
